g.text: atomic_t Correctness — Why plain int Breaks
atomic_t required: concurrent access from multiple CPUs
NOT per byte — call count only
3 | proc A write(42 bytes) | write_count: 0→1 bytes_used=42
4 | proc A read(10 bytes) | read_count: 0→1 f_pos: 0→10
5 | proc B read(10 bytes) | read_count: 1→2 f_pos: 0→10
6 | proc A read(10 bytes) | read_count: 2→3 f_pos: 10→20
7 | proc B write(0 bytes) | write_count: 1→2 (empty write)
N | snapshot for /proc | open_count=2 read=15 write=7
open_count | atomic_t | OK now — simple counter
read_count | atomic_t | OK now — simple counter
write_count | atomic_t | OK now — simple counter
buffer_data_len | size_t | NEEDS MUTEX — multi-step update
kernel_buffer | char* | NEEDS MUTEX — multi-step copy
buffer_size | size_t | NEEDS MUTEX — resize swaps ptr
Invariant: buffer_data_len <= buffer_size — must hold atomically

text.tspan: /* plain int — 3 machine ops, NOT atomic */
